name: Hungary
contextBlurb: Hungary applies the EU MiCA regime via Act VII of 2024: issuer rules for ART/EMT apply from 30 June 2024 and CASP authorisation from 30 December 2024, with a national transition ending 1 July 2025 after which CASPs must be authorised by the MNB. From 1 July 2025, crypto-to-fiat and crypto-to-crypto exchanges are lawful only if validated by an SZTFH-licensed “crypto-asset conversion validation service provider,” which issues a compliance certificate; exchanges without a certificate are invalid and can trigger criminal liability at values from HUF 5 million. Stablecoins are not legal tender and are treated under MiCA as e-money tokens (EMT) or asset-referenced tokens (ART).

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
SZTFH Decree 10/2025 (X.27.) publishes the validator (compliance-certificate) authorization procedure and requirements for crypto-asset conversions.
Hungary’s Criminal Code §394/A enters into force, penalising the use of unauthorised crypto-asset exchange services; users and providers must transact via authorised services.
MiCA’s stablecoin framework (Titles III & IV on ARTs/EMTs) becomes applicable EU-wide, covering issuers’ reserve, governance, disclosure, and redemption rules.
Remaining MiCA provisions, including CASP authorization and the recast TFR, become applicable across the EU.

keyFigures.name: Central Bank of Hungary (MNB)
keyFigures.role: MiCA competent authority (NCA) for Hungary; authorises and supervises CASPs and oversees ART/EMT issuance under MiCA.

keyFigures.role: Licences and supervises crypto-asset conversion validation service providers; their compliance certificate is required f

news.headline: Digital Watch – ESMA confirms end of MiCA transition period (April 2026)

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Hungary applies the EU MiCA regime via Act VII of 2024: issuer rules for ART/EMT apply from 30 June 2024 and CASP authorisation from 30 December 2024, with a national transition ending 1 July 2025 after which CASPs must be authorised by the MNB. From 1 July 2025, crypto-to-fiat and crypto-to-crypto exchanges are lawful only if validated by an SZTFH-licensed “crypto-asset conversion validation service provider,” which issues a compliance certificate; exchanges without a certificate are invalid and can trigger criminal liability at values from HUF 5 million. Stablecoins are not legal tender and are treated under MiCA as e-money tokens (EMT) or asset-referenced tokens (ART).
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: In Hungary, stablecoins are treated as crypto-assets under MiCA, implemented nationally by Act VII of 2024. They are not a separate legal category but fall under MiCA’s two types: asset-referenced tokens (ARTs) and e-money tokens (EMTs), with issuer authorisation and conduct rules set in MiCA and ov

regulators.name: Central Bank of Hungary
regulators.role: MiCA competent authority (NCA) for Hungary; authorises and supervises CASPs and oversees ART/EMT issuance under MiCA.

regulators.name: Supervisory Authority for Regulated Activities
regulators.role: Licences and supervises crypto-asset conversion validation service providers; their compliance certificate is required for exchange transactions under national law.
